perturbation.original_text: 2. Nondisclosure and Use of Confidential Information. Confidential Information shall not be used for any purpose other than to analyze, implement or complet e the Transactions . Confidential Information shall be held in strict confidence by Receiving Party and shall not be disclosed without prior written consent of Disclosing Party, except to those advisors, affiliates, agents, assigns, attorneys, employees, d irectors, officers and/or members (“Agents”) with a need -to-know the Confidential Information for the purposes of analyzing, implementing or completing the Transactions . Receiving Party shall require all recipients of the Confidential Information to be bo und by the terms of this Agreement. Receiving Party shall be responsible for any breach of this Agreement by the Receiving Party or its Agents. The Receiving Party shall use the same degree of care to protect the Confidential Information as the Receiving Party employs to protect its own information of like importance, but in no event less than a reasonable degree of care based on industry standard.
perturbation.changed_text: 2. Nondisclosure and Use of Confidential Information. Confidential Information, as defined herein, shall not be used for any purpose other than high-level evaluation of the Transactions. Confidential Information shall be held in strict confidence by Receiving Party and shall not be disclosed without prior written consent of Disclosing Party, except to a single designated advisor. Receiving Party shall require all recipients of the Confidential Information to be bo und by the terms of this Agreement. Receiving Party shall be responsible for any breach of this Agreement by the Receiving Party or its Agents. The Receiving Party shall use a minimal degree of care to protect the Confidential Information.
perturbation.explanation: This change restricts the use of Confidential Information to 'high-level evaluation' and limits disclosure to a 'single designated advisor'. This conflicts with Section 4, 'Remedies', which states that the Receiving Party's liability is limited to direct damages 'in connection with the evaluation of the Transactions'. The original Section 2 allows for 'analyzing, implementing or completing' the transaction, implying potential for extensive work and associated liability. By limiting use to 'high-level evaluation,' the actual activities covered by the liability limitation become vague and create an in-text contradiction, especially if Section 4 assumes a broader scope of activities beyond just 'high-level evaluation'. This in-text contradiction introduces uncertainty regarding the full extent of activities to which the limitation on liability applies.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 4. Remedies. The Receiving Party agrees that money damages would not be a sufficient remedy for any breach of this Agreement and that Disclosing Party shall be entitled to injunctive or other equitable relief to remedy or prevent any breach or threatened breach of this Agreement. Such remedy shall not be the exclusive remedy for any breach of this Agreement, but shall be in addition to all other rights and remedies available at law or in equity. Notwithstanding anything contained in this Agreement, the Receiving Party’s liability to the Disclosing Party in connection with this Agreement and any activities undertaken in connection with the evaluation of the Transactions shall be limite d to direct damages and shall exclude any other liability, including without limitation liability for special, indirect, punitive or consequential damages in contract, tort, warranty, strict liability or otherwise.
perturbation.justification: The changed text limits the use of Confidential Information to 'high-level evaluation' and disclosure to a 'single designated advisor'. This directly contradicts Section 4, which limits liability in connection with 'the evaluation of the Transactions' without specifying 'high-level'. The original text in Section 2 allows for 'analyzing, implementing or completing', implying a broader scope. By narrowing the permissible activities in the changed text while Section 4 refers to 'evaluation of the Transactions', it creates an in-text contradiction regarding the activities to which the liability limitation applies. This contradicts the original intent and creates ambiguity, fitting the Omissions - In Text Contradiction definition.

perturbation.original_text: 3. Required Disclosure. In the event that Receiving Party is requested or required by legal or regulatory a uthority to disclose any Confidential Information, the Receiving Party shall promptly notify the Disclosing Party of such request or requirement prior to disclosure, if permitted by law, so that Disclosing Party may seek an appropriate protective order. I n the event that a protective order or other remedy is not obtained, Receiving Party agrees to furnish only that portion of the Confidential Information that it reasonably determines, in consultation with its counsel, is consistent with the scope of the su bpoena or demand, and to exercise reasonable efforts to obtain assurance that confidential treatment will be accorded such Confidential Information. Receiving Party will provide reasonable cooperation to Disclosing Party and its legal counsel with respect to performance of the covenants undertaken pursuant to this Section 3.
perturbation.changed_text: 3. Required Disclosure. In the event that Receiving Party is requested or required by legal or regulatory authority to disclose any Confidential Information, the Receiving Party shall immediately disclose such information to comply with the request. Receiving Party will not provide cooperation to Disclosing Party and its legal counsel with respect to performance of the covenants undertaken pursuant to this Section 3.
perturbation.explanation: This perturbation removes the requirement for the Receiving Party to notify the Disclosing Party prior to disclosing Confidential Information when legally compelled and also removes the obligation to seek a protective order or confidential treatment. This contradicts Section 4 'Remedies'. By removing this protection, the risk of 'breach' (unauthorized disclosure) increases dramatically. But in Section 4 if the breach occurs because of the changed section, the section creates an in-text contradiction of not being protected by its 'Remedies'. While Section 4 still intends to provide remedies for breaches, it now covers breaches that the Receiving Party was forced to commit due to legal mandate, undermining the intent of the section.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 4. Remedies. The Receiving Party agrees that money damages would not be a sufficient remedy for any breach of this Agreement and that Disclosing Party shall be entitled to injunctive or other equitable relief to remedy or prevent any breach or threatened breach of this Agreement. Such remedy shall not be the exclusive remedy for any breach of this Agreement, but shall be in addition to all other rights and remedies available at law or in equity. Notwithstanding anything contained in this Agreement, the Receiving Party’s liability to the Disclosing Party in connection with this Agreement and any activities undertaken in connection with the evaluation of the Transactions shall be limite d to direct damages and shall exclude any other liability, including without limitation liability for special, indirect, punitive or consequential damages in contract, tort, warranty, strict liability or otherwise.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes the Receiving Party's obligation to notify the Disclosing Party before disclosing confidential information when legally required, and removes the requirement to seek a protective order. This creates a direct contradiction with Section 4 'Remedies', which assumes that the Disclosing Party has taken steps to prevent breaches. Now, breaches caused by legal mandates are not protected, directly undermining the intention of Section 4, which is to provide remedies against the Receiving Party for unauthorized disclosures, creating a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 5. Return or Destruction. Promptly fol lowing any decision by the Receiving Party not to continue discussions with respect to the Transactions , and at any other time upon the Disclosing Party’s written request, the Receiving Party shall return or destroy, at the Receiving Party’s option, all wr itten Confidential Information of the Disclosing Party, including that portion of such Confidential Information that may be found in analyses, compilations, studies or other documents prepared by, or for, the Receiving Party, and the Receiving Party and it s Agents shall not retain any copies of such written Confidential Information. At any time after which the Receiving Party has been required to return or destroy the Confidential Information in its possession in accordance with the preceding sentence, the Receiving Party shall, upon written request of the Disclosing Party, cause one of its duly authorized officers to certify in writing to the Disclosing Party that the requirements of the preceding sentence have Signal NDCA 20160308 3 been satisfied in full. The Receiving Party shall not be deemed to have retained or failed to destroy any Confidential Information which is an Imaged Document if such Confidential Information is deleted from local hard drives so long as no attempt is made to recover such Confidential Information fro m servers or back -up sources, provided that any such retained Confidential Information in an Imaged Document form shall remain subject to the disclosure and use restrictions set forth herein, notwithstanding any termination of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: 5. Return or Destruction. Following the termination of discussions, the Receiving Party shall return or destroy only the original documents provided by the Disclosing Party clearly marked 'Confidential'. The Receiving Party is not required to destroy analyses or derivative works. The Receiving Party shall not be deemed to have retained or failed to destroy any Confidential Information which is an Imaged Document if such Confidential Information is deleted from local hard drives so long as no attempt is made to recover such Confidential Information fro m servers or back -up sources, provided that any such retained Confidential Information in an Imaged Document form shall remain subject to the disclosure and use restrictions set forth herein, notwithstanding any termination of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: By limiting the return/destruction obligation to 'original documents provided by the Disclosing Party clearly marked 'Confidential'' and explicitly excluding 'analyses or derivative works', this creates an in-text contradiction with Section 8 'No License'. While Section 8 states that no license is granted to the Receiving Party, allowing the retention of derivative works created from the Confidential Information effectively grants a limited license to use that information, as they can use the analysis/derivative works. If a license were still required for that, the change causes an in-text contradiction that a license is in fact needed since they cannot use the analysis, creating the in-text contradiction that creates uncertainty.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 8. No License. It is understood that nothing contained in this A greement shall be construed as granting or conferring rights by license or otherwise in any Confidential Information disclosed to Receiving Party. Nothing in this Agreement is intended to prevent either Party hereto from using its own Confidential Informa tion which it furnished hereunder for dealings with third parties for any purpose.
perturbation.justification: The changed text limits the return/destruction obligation to 'original documents provided by the Disclosing Party clearly marked 'Confidential'' and explicitly excludes 'analyses or derivative works'. The contradicted text, Section 8 'No License', states that nothing in the agreement grants rights by license or otherwise in any Confidential Information disclosed to the Receiving Party. However, by allowing the Receiving Party to retain and use 'analyses or derivative works', the changed text implicitly grants a license to use the Confidential Information, which is in direct conflict with Section 8. The original text required the Receiving Party to return or destroy, at the Receiving Party’s option, all written Confidential Information of the Disclosing Party including that portion of such Confidential Information that may be found in analyses, compilations, studies or other documents prepared by, or for, the Receiving Party, thus the change creates a contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 7. No Other Agreement. It is understood that this Agreement is not intended to and does not, obligate either Party to e nter into any further agreements or to proceed with any possible relationship or other transaction, including without limitation the Transactions , or to require either Party to disclose of any information under this Agreement. Any pricing lists, proposals or summaries disclosed under this Agreement (“Pricing Documents”) are intended only to provide a framework for further discussions between the Parties. Pricing Documents are not an offer or a commitment of either Party . Neither Party will be bound by an y of the terms of these Pricing Documents unless and until the final terms are incorporated into a mutually agreed, final definitive agreement that is executed and delivered by both Parties.
perturbation.changed_text: 7. No Other Agreement. It is understood that this Agreement is not intended to and does not, obligate either Party to enter into any further agreements or to proceed with any possible relationship or other transaction, including without limitation the Transactions, or to require either Party to disclose of any information under this Agreement. Any pricing lists, proposals or summaries disclosed under this Agreement (“Pricing Documents”) are a binding offer and commitment of both Parties. Neither Party will be bound by any of the terms of these Pricing Documents unless and until the final terms are incorporated into a mutually agreed, final definitive agreement that is executed and delivered by both Parties.
perturbation.explanation: This change makes 'Pricing Documents' a 'binding offer and commitment,' which directly contradicts the statement that the agreement does not obligate either party to enter into further agreements or proceed with the transaction. This creates an in-text contradiction. By making the Pricing Documents binding, it creates a commitment that violates the initial statement of the section because of the change made. It now creates an in-text contradiction within the same section, causing uncertainty for enforcement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 7. No Other Agreement. It is understood that this Agreement is not intended to and does not, obligate either Party to e nter into any further agreements or to proceed with any possible relationship or other transaction, including without limitation the Transactions , or to require either Party to disclose of any information under this Agreement. Any pricing lists, proposals or summaries disclosed under this Agreement (“Pricing Documents”) are a binding offer and commitment of both Parties. Neither Party will be bound by an y of the terms of these Pricing Documents unless and until the final terms are incorporated into a mutually agreed, final definitive agreement that is executed and delivered by both Parties.
perturbation.justification: The changed text makes 'Pricing Documents' a 'binding offer and commitment,' which directly contradicts the initial statement in the same section that the agreement does not obligate either party to enter into further agreements or proceed with the transaction. This creates an in-text contradiction, because the contradicted text is the same as the changed text and the original text said that Pricing Documents are not an offer or commitment of either Party . The changed text directly contradicts this earlier statement.

perturbation.original_text: 13. Term. This Agreement shall terminate three (3 ) year s following written notice by either Party to the other of its desire to terminate th is Agreement. However, the obligations contained herein shall remain in effect for a period of five (5) years from the date the Confidential Information was disclosed under this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: 13. Term. This Agreement shall terminate upon the completion of the Transaction. However, the obligations contained herein shall remain in effect until such time that the Receiving Party no longer has access to Confidential Information.
perturbation.explanation: Changing the term to terminate 'upon the completion of the Transaction' and continuing obligations only 'until such time that the Receiving Party no longer has access to Confidential Information' creates a contradiction with Section 17, 'Waiver of Trial by Jury'. The original agreement had a defined term, but if there is no way to make sure the Receiving Party no longer has access to Confidential Information, it makes the 'Waiver of Trial by Jury' almost indefinite since a trial could be brough anytime if the access to information is not provable. If that cannot be verified, the term could last forever. If the jury is waived forever that causes an in-text contradiction of an forever waiving of the jury.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 17. Waiver of Trial by Jury. EACH PARTY HEREBY KNOWINGLY, VOLUNTARILY AND INTENTIONALLY WAIVES THE RIGHT EI THER OF THEM MAY HAVE TO A TRIAL BY JURY IN RESPECT OF ANY LITIGATION BASED HEREON, OR ARISING OUT OF, UNDER OR IN CONNECTION WITH THIS AGREEMENT. THIS PROVISION IS A MATERIAL INDUCEMENT FOR THE PARTIES ENTERING INTO THIS AGREEMENT.
perturbation.justification: The original text specified a termination date three years after notice. The changed text replaces this with termination upon completion of the transaction and obligations ending when the receiving party no longer has access to confidential information. This creates a potential indefinite term, which directly contradicts Section 17, which involves a waiver of jury trial. If the obligations can extend indefinitely because access to information cannot be proven, the jury waiver could also be indefinite. This contradiction significantly impacts the enforceability and understanding of the agreement, as it introduces an indefinite term where the original had a definite term. Thus this causes an in-text contradiction because the contract sections contradict each other regarding obligations and rights of each party.
